stories.title: Das neue Eintracht-Auswärtstrikot
stories.summary: Der Adler der 80er und 90er ist zurück – ohne je wirklich weg gewesen zu sein. Historisches Wappen im Mittelpunkt Das historische Wappen steht im Mittelpunkt und macht ein einzigartiges Zeitgefühl wieder erlebbar – für alle, die Dekaden der 80er und 90er selbst erlebt haben, aber ebenso für alle Eintracht-Fans, die sich über Retro-Ästhetik und Erzählungen mit dieser Ära verbunden fühlen. Gleichzeitig knüpft das neue Jersey an besondere Ereignisse der Vergangenheit an: In Weiß sind die Mannschaften der Eintracht immer wieder über sich hinausgewachsen. Auf internationaler Bühne. In Finalspielen. Vor einer weißen Kurve.

stories.title: Muere el ganadero Luis López cuando buscaban unas reses que se le habían escapado en un pueblo de Valencia
stories.summary: Luis López, un ganadero de 30 años de Segorbe, murió este miércoles tras sentirse indispuesto mientras buscaba reses escapadas en Gátova. El joven, dueño de una ganadería de toros bravos inscrita en la Agrupación Española de Ganaderos de Reses Bravas, llevaba seis años al frente de su explotación. Las autoridades locales han transmitido su pésame a la familia.

stories.title: Former River City actor jailed for nine years for rape and assault
stories.summary: The trial heard how Robertson, of Carbeth in Stirlingshire, raped a woman on one occasion between 2018 and 2019 as well as a separate sex assault. The pair came to know each other from working together on River City. The woman said she did not want anything sexual to happen, but believed Robertson did. The relationship became intimate, but the woman later told Robertson she wanted their relationship to go back to being platonic. She said one evening she was at his home in Glasgow's Tradeston and planned to sleep on the sofa, but Robertson allegedly told her she could join him in bed as "nothing would happen". However, the witness said that the actor instead "had sex" with her.

stories.title: Großeinsatz in Stuttgart: Person schwer verletzt aufgefunden
stories.summary: ANZEIGE Nachrichten Panorama Aus aller Welt Täter auf der Flucht Person in Stuttgarter Einkaufszentrum schwer verletzt Ein Einsatzfahrzeug der Polizei fährt mit Blaulicht durch die Innenstadt. Jens Büttner/dpa FOCUS-online-Redaktion Donnerstag , 23.07.2026 , 11:31 Großeinsatz der Polizei in Stuttgart am Donnerstagvormittag. In einem Einkaufszentrum in der Innenstadt ist eine Person schwer verletzt worden. Nach Auskunft der Polizei ist der unbekannte Täter flüchtig und wird gesucht. Der Rettungsdienst versorge die verletzte Person. Es bestehe keine Gefahr für die Bevölkerung. Das Einkaufszentrum sei teilweise abgesperrt. Polizei und Feuerwehr seien mit starken Kräften vor Ort.

stories.title: «Nous ne sommes plus ensemble, mais toujours mariés et vivant sous le même toit» : Jade Lagardère répond aux questions sur son couple
stories.summary: Jade Lagardère announced her separation from husband Arnaud Lagardère during an Instagram Q&A on Wednesday, stating, "We are no longer a couple." Despite their split, the couple remains married and continues to live together for the sake of their three children, emphasizing their commitment to family well-being and mutual respect. Jade also revealed ongoing health challenges, having faced multiple hospitalizations since last year.

stories.title: Hoe staat het nieuwe Ajax ervoor? Coach Míchel begint net als Farioli tegen Vojvodina
stories.summary: Ajax kicks off its new season tonight away to Vojvodina in the Conference League second qualifying round, marking the first official match under coach Míchel. The Dutch side, missing injured defenders Youri Baas and Dies Janse, spent over 30 million euros on new signings including striker Marcos Leonardo and left-back Caio Henrique. Míchel’s side faces Vojvodina at 20:00 CET, with the return leg in Amsterdam next Thursday.

stories.title: La reacción de Gerard Piqué al preguntarle por la actuación de Shakira al regresar a España con sus hijos Milan y Sasha
stories.summary: Gerard Piqué no se perdió la gran final del Mundial de fútbol 2026. El deportista estuvo en las gradas del MetLife Stadium de Nueva Jersey junto a sus hijos, Milan y Sasha, y no solo presenció la victoria de España frente a Argentina, sino también la actuación de su expareja, Shakira, durante el medio tiempo del partido. Un detalle que ha sido muy comentado, pues todos estaban pendientes de su reacción. Tras su paso por Estados Unidos, el exfutbolista ya está de vuelta en España. Gerard Piqué ha aterrizado en el aeropuerto de Barcelona junto a sus hijos, Milan y Sasha, de 13 y 11 años, respectivamente.

stories.title: "Se non paghi ti brucio il locale", poi la testata al poliziotto e la fuga finita a Viterbo
stories.summary: A 44-year-old man was jailed in Viterbo after a violent assault on a police officer in Anzio on July 10. He tried to extort money from a shopkeeper, threatened to burn down the store, then fractured a policeman’s nose with a headbutt during arrest. The officer received 30 days’ medical leave while the suspect, already known to local traders, remains under investigation.

stories.title: Former Formula 3 driver Zdeněk Chovanec has died aged 21
stories.summary: The motorsport community is mourning the loss of Zdeněk Chovanec. It has now been revealed that the 21-year-old racing driver passed away last Saturday at the age of 21. Chovanec died in Trofa, Portugal. Born in Venezuela, Chovanec had Czech roots and had lived in Portugal since his youth; he spent most of his career racing under a Portuguese licence and was regarded as a promising young talent in the country’s motorsport scene. Zdeněk Chovanec began his career in 2018 in karting in Portugal, before quickly moving into formula racing. In 2020, he competed in the United Arab Emirates Formula 4 series and the Italian Formula 4 Championship, where he made a name for himself with strong results.

stories.title: Een wit verkeerslicht: zo werkt het en dit moet je doen
stories.summary: In Rome duikt een nieuw verkeerslicht op: wit. Het is geen vervanger van rood, oranje en groen, maar een extra signaal speciaal ontwikkeld voor kruispunten met zelfrijdende auto’s. Zodra het witte licht brandt, nemen autonome voertuigen de regie over. Zij “overleggen” digitaal wie wanneer mag gaan, zodat het verkeer vloeiender doorstroomt dan met de klassieke lichtcycli. Voor gewone bestuurders verandert er dan iets essentieels: je volgt niet meer het stoplicht, maar het gedrag van de auto voor je. Remt die, dan rem jij; rijdt die door, dan kun je veilig meelopen in de stroom.

stories.title: Corruzione, chieste misure cautelari per il sindaco e il professionista
stories.summary: Il sindaco Pasquale Marrandino ‘Mazzette’ in cambio di incarichi pubblici ben remunerati. È quanto contestato dai sostituti procuratori Giacomo Urbano e Anna Ida Capone al sindaco di Castel Volturno Pasquale Marrandino e all’ingegnere Daniele De Caprio indagati per corruzione. Una scatolina bianca passata dal professionista all’interno della Jeep del primo cittadino e nascosta sotto il sedile e poi riaperta più volte per prelevare le banconote. E’ l’immagine ‘chiave’ dell’inchiesta dei carabinieri del nucleo operativo di Caserta che sette mesi fa ha portato a perquisizioni nelle abitazioni, uffici e automobili dei due indagati.

stories.title: 6 Emmerdale spoilers for tonight confirm Dr Todd death suspect
stories.summary: Emmerdale spoilers follow from Thursday's episode (23 July), which is available to watch now on ITVX and YouTube but hasn't aired on ITV1 yet.In tonight's Emmerdale, Vanessa and Mackenzie share their frustration over the lack of justice in Charity's case against Dr Todd. But one of them seems determined to take control of the situation…Meanwhile, Cain is furious when his camping trip with Kyle is hijacked by Graham – and the simmering tension has worrying consequences.Read on for six big moments from Thursday's episode…1. Mack tries to move onIt's a new day for Charity after Helen's decision not to go to the police with a formal complaint against Dr Todd.

stories.title: Suore lasciano dopo 75 anni, chiude la comunità delle Orsoline: «Crisi di vocazioni, mancano religiose disponibili»
stories.summary: Per settantacinque anni sono state una presenza discreta ma costante. Hanno visto crescere generazioni di bambini, accompagnato famiglie nei momenti di gioia e di dolore, insegnato il catechismo, pregato, ascoltato e aiutato chi bussava alla loro porta. Da settembre tutto questo diventerà un ricordo. Le Suore Orsoline Figlie di Maria Immacolata lasceranno Castelmassa, segnando un altro addio che colpisce il Polesine dopo la recente partenza delle religiose da Scardovari. Un epilogo che racconta non solo la fine di una lunga storia, ma anche la crisi vocazionale che sta cambiando il volto della Chiesa.

stories.title: Guardiola verso il no all'Italia: vuole 20 milioni l'anno, la corsa al ct si riapre
stories.summary: Per un’oretta ha potuto staccare la mente dal pensiero del ct. Giovanni Malagò ieri sera, a un passo dal Tevere, ha accantonato l’abito presidenziale, indossato la maglia del “suo” Circolo Aniene che ha guidato alla vittoria della Coppa Canottieri, torneo storico tra i circoli romani. Accanto a lui Roberto Mancini, autore di cinque gol, ma questa è un’altra storia. Malagò aspetta e ovviamente spera. E, ormai è cosa nota, l’uomo che vorrebbe tanto portare sulla panchina azzurra è Pep Guardiola. Quello per cui "si possono anche fare eccezioni" persino se si parla di ingaggi, nonostante il budget federale sia un problema dichiarato.

stories.title: Ashes 2027 schedule: England vs Australia men's series to start at Trent Bridge, women's at Headingley
stories.summary: England’s men’s Ashes campaign opens at Trent Bridge on 18 June 2027, with the women’s series starting at Headingley six days later. The men’s Tests will also be played at Lord’s, Edgbaston, Southampton and the Oval, while the women’s matches run from 24 June to 20 July. Both series will run concurrently as the teams chase Ashes glory on home soil.

stories.title: Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston and Kelsey Mitchell make WNBA history before All-Star Weekend
stories.summary: Caitlin Clark led the Indiana Fever to a historic 123-88 victory over the Connecticut Sun, marking the team's highest single-game score and tying for the third-highest in WNBA history. Clark scored 27 points and dished out 11 assists, while Kelsey Mitchell and Aliyah Boston added 23 and 22 points, respectively, making them all starters for the upcoming All-Star game. The Fever, now 17-10, head into the All-Star break on a three-game winning streak.

stories.title: Omicidio e tentato femminicidio a Quartu e Solanas, spara uccidendo il fratello e ferendo gravemente la moglie
stories.summary: Nel giro di poche ore ci sarebbero stati un omicidio e un tentato femminicidio nel Cagliaritano e il responsabile sarebbe la stessa persona. La vittima sarebbe un uomo di 43 anni, ucciso a Quartu. Prima di lui, il responsabile avrebbe ferito gravemente una donna di 48 anni a Solanas. Si sarebbe poi costituito alle forze dell’ordine, che interrogandolo avrebbero confermato che i due delitti sono collegati. Secondo le prime ricostruzioni, le vittime sarebbero la moglie e il fratello dell’uomo che si è costituito.

stories.title: Virginie Efira se livre sur sa seconde grossesse à 46 ans: "Je ne pensais vraiment plus avoir d'enfant"
stories.summary: Virginie Efira est une maman épanouie. Aujourd'hui en couple avec le comédien Niels Schneider , l'actrice belge est à la tête d'une famille de deux enfants. Le premier, Ali , est né en 2013 de sa relation avec le réalisateur Mabrouk El Mechri. Le second, Hiro , a lui été conçu dix ans plus tard avec son compagnon actuel. Pourtant, la native de Schaerbeek a longtemps cru construire sa vie avec une fille unique. La deuxième grossesse de la star de Revoir Paris n'était, en effet, pas prévue, comme elle l'explique dans Psychologie magazine. "J'avais 46 ans quand mon fils est né, je pensais vraiment ne plus avoir d'enfant. C'était une merveilleuse surprise" , confie-t-elle.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026: Gary Anderson thrashes Ryan Joyce to reach last-16
stories.summary: Gary Anderson dominated Ryan Joyce with a 10-2 victory to advance to the last-16 of the World Matchplay Darts in Blackpool. The 2018 champion showcased his skill with impressive finishes of 144 and 141, averaging nearly 110. Anderson will face Welsh fifth seed Jonny Clayton next, who defeated Damon Heta 10-7.

stories.title: Commissioner encourages reporting as concern over antisocial behaviour continues
stories.summary: Police and Crime Commissioner Alison Hernandez reports rising antisocial behaviour concerns in Devon and Cornwall towns like Looe, Brixham and Exmouth. Over the past year, authorities have issued 31 closure orders, 40 Community Protection Warnings and more than 40 Notices to address persistent nuisance. Today’s panel in Plymouth will review ongoing legal interventions to protect communities across the peninsula.

stories.title: ClickHouse announced as Principal Partner & Front of Shirt Sponsor
stories.summary: Fulham Football Club is proud to announce ClickHouse, a global technology company specialising in real-time analytics, data infrastructure and AI applications, as its newest Principal Partner and Front of Shirt Sponsor of the Men’s First Team.

stories.title: Vollsperrung auf der A1 - Lkw mit Kakaobohnen steht in Flammen
stories.summary: The A1 near Seevetal in Lower Saxony remains fully closed after a truck carrying cocoa beans caught fire around 9 a.m. today. The driver escaped unharmed but Glutnester in the cargo are still being extinguished, delaying reopening. Authorities expect prolonged southbound restrictions until the blaze is fully under control.

stories.title: HandOfBlood will not be at gamescom 2026, the reason meets with much agreement: "Used to be a real games fair"
stories.summary: Top German gaming influencer Max “HandOfBlood” Knabe will miss gamescom 2026, his management confirms. Influencer crowd chaos at past events—like MontanaBlack’s 2022 appearance—prompted the move. Organizers cite stricter accreditation rules as the fair shifts back to games over influencer spectacle.

stories.title: River levels approach all-time low as drought announcement looms
stories.summary: River levels in Wales are approaching record lows as drought conditions intensify, with July rainfall at just 5% of the average amid ongoing heatwaves. Natural Resources Wales is set to confirm an environmental drought for parts of the country later today. Farmers report failing crops and heat-stressed livestock as wildlife suffers from critically low river flows.

stories.title: 'Ter Stegens komst is kwestie van tijd; Ounahi blijkt toch best wel lastig verhaal'
stories.summary: Ajax expects Marc-André ter Stegen’s move to be finalized soon, with the German goalkeeper still linked to the Amsterdam club. Negotiations between Ter Stegen and Barcelona remain the final hurdle, delaying Ajax’s defensive rebuild. The club remains confident a deal will be completed despite ongoing fiscal discussions.

stories.title: The music in Nolan’s Odyssey is as well-crafted – and bloodlessly unmemorable – as the movie itself
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey features a score by Ludwig Göransson that draws on 2,000-year-old Greek musical research. The soundtrack’s aulos theme blends reconstructed ancient melodies with modern film scoring. Critics note the music enhances the film’s epic scale without overshadowing its visuals.

stories.title: Darwin Núñez acelera su salida de Saudí con la vista puesta en el Barça
stories.summary: El delantero Darwin Núñez está ya forzando la máquina para salir del Al Hilal en este mercado de fichajes. Ni el club saudí cuenta con él, ni el jugador uruguayo está por la labor de continuar en un equipo que ha apostado claramente por Karim Benzema dejándole en el banquillo. Núñez se incorporó este martes a la concentración del equipo en Austria y espera solventar su situación antes del 3 de agosto, día en el que todo el grupo debe regresar ya para Arabia.

stories.title: Third person dies from NYC Legionnaires’ outbreak
stories.summary: A third person has died from the Legionnaires’ disease outbreak on New York City’s Upper East Side, health officials confirmed Sunday. The cluster has sickened at least 74 people, with eight still hospitalized as of Sunday night. Officials say the decline in new cases suggests the source of the outbreak has likely been contained.

stories.title: Le nouveau Rafale F5 et le grand drone de combat, priorités de Dassault Aviation
stories.summary: Dassault Aviation confirms the Rafale F5 and a new combat drone as top priorities after France and Germany ended the Future Air Combat System project. The NGF fighter jet, once central to the program, will now proceed independently with interoperability for allied aircraft. Development on the combat cloud component has been halted following the joint decision last month.

stories.title: 'Even the CEO doesn't know' - Kaymer on LIV's future
stories.summary: Martin Kaymer says LIV Golf’s 2024 team championship in Michigan is “highly unlikely” as Saudi backing ends this season after £3.75bn of investment. CEO Scott O’Neil is seeking $350m to keep the tour alive but admits even he lacks clarity on 2027 plans. The next event starts Thursday at JCB Club in Staffordshire ahead of stops in New York and Indianapolis.

stories.title: Honourable defeat might be the best Mayo can hope for against Kerry
stories.summary: In the aftermath of last Sunday , we gathered and tried to pinpoint just where exactly this lay on the ever-growing list of All-Ireland final defeats we’d seen Galway suffer. We cycled through some options. Nothing will ever be worse than the 2024 football final – that’s a given (our generation’s 1983). The supremacy of Limerick in every facet of last Sunday’s hurling decider meant there was no one moment you could look at as a turning point. There was no disputed late free, as there was for the footballers in the 2022 final against Kerry, for instance.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a landmark nuclear cooperation deal on Wednesday, allowing Riyadh to develop a civilian nuclear program with expanded access for American companies. The agreement includes a bilateral safeguards pact to prevent proliferation, though details remain undisclosed. Experts warn the deal could set a precedent for uranium enrichment in the region.

stories.title: Daniel Wu on ‘Cold War 1994,’ ‘Twilight of the Warriors’ and Coming Home to Hong Kong: ‘The Craft Still Challenges Me’
stories.summary: Daniel Wu stars in Cold War 1994, the third installment of the highest-grossing Hong Kong franchise, premiering at the New York Asian Film Festival. The prequel, set before the 1997 handover, reunites Wu with three generations of Hong Kong actors. Wu returns to Hong Kong filmmaking after pandemic delays, calling the production’s scale and script a key draw.

stories.title: Framework’s premium laptop is shipping with less RAM
stories.summary: Framework is raising prices and adjusting RAM on its Laptop 13 Pro after a supplier cost surge, with prebuilt models now starting at $1,599. Some preorder customers face revised memory configurations and higher prices, though Framework offers full refunds for cancellations. New orders reflect the updated pricing and specs.

stories.title: UK millionaires ask Andy Burnham to tax them more
stories.summary: UK millionaires including Gary Lineker and Brian Eno have urged Prime Minister Andy Burnham to raise taxes on the wealthiest. A group of 120 signatories proposed a 2% tax on fortunes over £10m to fund public services. Burnham has not ruled out a wealth tax but has given no further details.

stories.title: Venice Film Festival Reveals 2026 Lineup (Updating Live)
stories.summary: The Venice Film Festival unveiled its 2026 lineup today, opening with Danny Boyle’s “Ink,” a biographical drama about Rupert Murdoch starring Guy Pearce and Jack O’Connell. Netflix acquired U.S. rights to the film, which will compete for the Golden Lion. Italian director Giovanni Veronesi’s “Dio Ride” closes the festival outside the competition.

stories.title: No 2027 men's Ashes Test in north of England
stories.summary: England’s men’s Ashes series against Australia next year will begin at Trent Bridge on 18 June, with the women’s series starting six days later. The men’s Tests will run through July at Lord’s, Edgbaston, Southampton and the Oval, but no matches are scheduled in the north of England. England will open their campaign with a Test against Bangladesh at Lord’s from 28 May.

stories.title: No men's Ashes Test in north of England - full 2027 schedule
stories.summary: England’s men’s Ashes campaign starts at Trent Bridge on 18 June 2027, with no Tests in the north of England after Old Trafford and Headingley were left out. The five-Test series runs alongside the women’s Ashes from 24 June to 20 July, ending at the Kia Oval on 29 July. Australia will warm up against England Lions at Old Trafford from 7 June before the opening Test.

stories.title: Kürzungen bei armen Rentnern und Familien: Verband warnt vor Regierungsplänen
stories.summary: Die Bundesregierung plant Kürzungen beim Wohngeld, die vor allem Familien mit Kindern, Geringverdiener und Rentner mit kleinen Renten treffen würden. Laut einer Kurzexpertise des Paritätischen Wohlfahrtsverbands wären 70 Prozent der Wohngeldempfänger Haushalte mit Kindern und 15 Prozent Rentner. Betroffene warnen, dass weitere Kürzungen die ohnehin prekäre finanzielle Situation verschärfen würden.

stories.title: Al Hilal open talks to sign Ousmane Dembélé from PSG
stories.summary: Al Hilal has initiated talks to sign Ousmane Dembélé from Paris Saint-Germain as part of their strategy to enhance their squad during the summer transfer window. The Saudi club has identified the 28-year-old forward as a key target and discussions with his representatives are already underway. Meanwhile, PSG is keen to extend Dembélé's contract, emphasizing their desire to retain the player who has become central to their attacking lineup.

stories.title: ‘He knows what he’s doing’: Burnham’s off-the-cuff remarks inspire headlines and headaches
stories.summary: Britain’s Prime Minister Andy Burnham sparked fresh debate on Wednesday after suggesting higher earners may face tax rises while ruling out an income tax threshold increase. His off-the-cuff remarks came just three days into office, testing his promise to do politics differently. Downing Street is expected to clarify the policy platform by the end of the week.

stories.title: Around 12,000 people evacuated in France fires
stories.summary: Around 12,000 people have been evacuated in France’s Gironde region as wildfires rage, with over 2,000 hectares burned. No injuries have been reported, though authorities have banned engine use in forested areas and closed roads. Meanwhile, Italy mourns a firefighter killed battling fires in Sicily, where more than 350 blazes have prompted evacuations.

stories.title: The millennial generation has split, new Fed research shows: those over 35 are edging toward boomer-style wealth, while everyone else falls behind
stories.summary: Millennials over 35 are closing in on boomer-era homeownership levels, but those under 35 now own homes at just 22%, according to new Federal Reserve research. The traditional homeownership rate of 37% for under-35s only counts household heads, masking the true gap. The Fed’s new measure shows national homeownership at 53%, far below the widely cited 65%.

stories.title: Government to cut business rates for pubs, clubs and music venues
stories.summary: Pubs, clubs and music venues in England will receive a 20% cut to business rates from April, saving firms around £1,100 each next year. The government estimates the move will support nearly 32,000 venues at a cost of £100m. Details on eligibility, excluding the largest live music venues, will be confirmed at the autumn Budget.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Deal Prompts Fears of a Nuclear Arms Race
stories.summary: In Europe, Asia and the Middle East, countries seek nuclear capabilities to hedge against aggressive neighbors and a receding Washington, potentially creating a more freewheeling era.

stories.title: A New Target for Ukraine’s Long-Range Attacks: Russia’s Version of Amazon
stories.summary: Kyiv is escalating its air war by striking commercial warehouses, trying to bring the conflict closer to home for ordinary Russians.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

stories.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
stories.summary: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
stories.summary: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

stories.title: As Cyclosporiasis Spreads, America Says No Thanks to Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
stories.summary: It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Alphabet reported a negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion in its latest quarter, its first deficit in over a decade, as AI infrastructure costs surged to $45 billion. Revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but stock fell 4% after hours. The company now expects AI spending to reach $205 billion this year.

stories.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
stories.summary: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike today, losing 11 kilograms after demanding government action on education reforms. Protests over exam paper leaks have intensified pressure on Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government, which now faces calls to address systemic failures. Authorities say fast-track courts will be set up to punish those responsible for leaks.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukrainian drone strikes have hit two Wildberries warehouses in Russia, killing eight workers and disrupting supplies for hundreds of small businesses. The attacks in Elektrostal and Kotovsk follow Kyiv’s shift to targeting logistics hubs used by Russia’s military. Thousands of sellers now face financial losses as the war’s impact spreads closer to home.

stories.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
stories.summary: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station captured a stunning panorama of Washington state’s Olympic Mountains on July 23, 2026. The image highlights the rugged peaks, deep river valleys, and alpine glaciers of Olympic National Park. The region’s unique geology, formed from ancient undersea eruptions, remains a remote wilderness surrounded by coastal waters and islands.

stories.title: Butterfly city blooms in shadow of office blocks
stories.summary: A mini meadow in Hull city centre has become a haven for ten butterfly species, including the rare small skipper. The project, launched in 2023 near St Stephen’s Shopping Centre, allows grass and wildflowers to grow undisturbed to support caterpillars. Organisers plan to expand the scheme to more urban sites next year.

stories.title: Alarm over hospital lung admissions for children in England’s poorest postcodes
stories.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections than those in wealthier postcodes, new NHS data shows. In 2024/25, 1,941 per 100,000 children in the most deprived areas were admitted compared to 1,380 per 100,000 in the least deprived. Experts warn air pollution and deprivation are key drivers, with calls for urgent action to address the disparity.

stories.title: Life-changing MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
stories.summary: The NHS in England will now offer fampridine, the first drug to help some people with multiple sclerosis walk, to up to 5,000 patients annually. The treatment, which boosts nerve signals to improve mobility, follows clinical trials showing walking benefits for four in ten users. NHS England confirmed the drug’s inclusion after private costs became unsustainable for many patients.

offbeat.title: Das neue Eintracht-Auswärtstrikot
offbeat.summary: Der Adler der 80er und 90er ist zurück – ohne je wirklich weg gewesen zu sein. Historisches Wappen im Mittelpunkt Das historische Wappen steht im Mittelpunkt und macht ein einzigartiges Zeitgefühl wieder erlebbar – für alle, die Dekaden der 80er und 90er selbst erlebt haben, aber ebenso für alle Eintracht-Fans, die sich über Retro-Ästhetik und Erzählungen mit dieser Ära verbunden fühlen. Gleichzeitig knüpft das neue Jersey an besondere Ereignisse der Vergangenheit an: In Weiß sind die Mannschaften der Eintracht immer wieder über sich hinausgewachsen. Auf internationaler Bühne. In Finalspielen. Vor einer weißen Kurve.

offbeat.title: Muere el ganadero Luis López cuando buscaban unas reses que se le habían escapado en un pueblo de Valencia
offbeat.summary: Luis López, un ganadero de 30 años de Segorbe, murió este miércoles tras sentirse indispuesto mientras buscaba reses escapadas en Gátova. El joven, dueño de una ganadería de toros bravos inscrita en la Agrupación Española de Ganaderos de Reses Bravas, llevaba seis años al frente de su explotación. Las autoridades locales han transmitido su pésame a la familia.

offbeat.title: Former River City actor jailed for nine years for rape and assault
offbeat.summary: The trial heard how Robertson, of Carbeth in Stirlingshire, raped a woman on one occasion between 2018 and 2019 as well as a separate sex assault. The pair came to know each other from working together on River City. The woman said she did not want anything sexual to happen, but believed Robertson did. The relationship became intimate, but the woman later told Robertson she wanted their relationship to go back to being platonic. She said one evening she was at his home in Glasgow's Tradeston and planned to sleep on the sofa, but Robertson allegedly told her she could join him in bed as "nothing would happen". However, the witness said that the actor instead "had sex" with her.

offbeat.title: Großeinsatz in Stuttgart: Person schwer verletzt aufgefunden
offbeat.summary: ANZEIGE Nachrichten Panorama Aus aller Welt Täter auf der Flucht Person in Stuttgarter Einkaufszentrum schwer verletzt Ein Einsatzfahrzeug der Polizei fährt mit Blaulicht durch die Innenstadt. Jens Büttner/dpa FOCUS-online-Redaktion Donnerstag , 23.07.2026 , 11:31 Großeinsatz der Polizei in Stuttgart am Donnerstagvormittag. In einem Einkaufszentrum in der Innenstadt ist eine Person schwer verletzt worden. Nach Auskunft der Polizei ist der unbekannte Täter flüchtig und wird gesucht. Der Rettungsdienst versorge die verletzte Person. Es bestehe keine Gefahr für die Bevölkerung. Das Einkaufszentrum sei teilweise abgesperrt. Polizei und Feuerwehr seien mit starken Kräften vor Ort.

offbeat.title: «Nous ne sommes plus ensemble, mais toujours mariés et vivant sous le même toit» : Jade Lagardère répond aux questions sur son couple
offbeat.summary: Jade Lagardère announced her separation from husband Arnaud Lagardère during an Instagram Q&A on Wednesday, stating, "We are no longer a couple." Despite their split, the couple remains married and continues to live together for the sake of their three children, emphasizing their commitment to family well-being and mutual respect. Jade also revealed ongoing health challenges, having faced multiple hospitalizations since last year.

offbeat.title: Hoe staat het nieuwe Ajax ervoor? Coach Míchel begint net als Farioli tegen Vojvodina
offbeat.summary: Ajax kicks off its new season tonight away to Vojvodina in the Conference League second qualifying round, marking the first official match under coach Míchel. The Dutch side, missing injured defenders Youri Baas and Dies Janse, spent over 30 million euros on new signings including striker Marcos Leonardo and left-back Caio Henrique. Míchel’s side faces Vojvodina at 20:00 CET, with the return leg in Amsterdam next Thursday.

offbeat.title: La reacción de Gerard Piqué al preguntarle por la actuación de Shakira al regresar a España con sus hijos Milan y Sasha
offbeat.summary: Gerard Piqué no se perdió la gran final del Mundial de fútbol 2026. El deportista estuvo en las gradas del MetLife Stadium de Nueva Jersey junto a sus hijos, Milan y Sasha, y no solo presenció la victoria de España frente a Argentina, sino también la actuación de su expareja, Shakira, durante el medio tiempo del partido. Un detalle que ha sido muy comentado, pues todos estaban pendientes de su reacción. Tras su paso por Estados Unidos, el exfutbolista ya está de vuelta en España. Gerard Piqué ha aterrizado en el aeropuerto de Barcelona junto a sus hijos, Milan y Sasha, de 13 y 11 años, respectivamente.

offbeat.title: "Se non paghi ti brucio il locale", poi la testata al poliziotto e la fuga finita a Viterbo
offbeat.summary: A 44-year-old man was jailed in Viterbo after a violent assault on a police officer in Anzio on July 10. He tried to extort money from a shopkeeper, threatened to burn down the store, then fractured a policeman’s nose with a headbutt during arrest. The officer received 30 days’ medical leave while the suspect, already known to local traders, remains under investigation.

offbeat.title: Former Formula 3 driver Zdeněk Chovanec has died aged 21
offbeat.summary: The motorsport community is mourning the loss of Zdeněk Chovanec. It has now been revealed that the 21-year-old racing driver passed away last Saturday at the age of 21. Chovanec died in Trofa, Portugal. Born in Venezuela, Chovanec had Czech roots and had lived in Portugal since his youth; he spent most of his career racing under a Portuguese licence and was regarded as a promising young talent in the country’s motorsport scene. Zdeněk Chovanec began his career in 2018 in karting in Portugal, before quickly moving into formula racing. In 2020, he competed in the United Arab Emirates Formula 4 series and the Italian Formula 4 Championship, where he made a name for himself with strong results.

offbeat.title: Een wit verkeerslicht: zo werkt het en dit moet je doen
offbeat.summary: In Rome duikt een nieuw verkeerslicht op: wit. Het is geen vervanger van rood, oranje en groen, maar een extra signaal speciaal ontwikkeld voor kruispunten met zelfrijdende auto’s. Zodra het witte licht brandt, nemen autonome voertuigen de regie over. Zij “overleggen” digitaal wie wanneer mag gaan, zodat het verkeer vloeiender doorstroomt dan met de klassieke lichtcycli. Voor gewone bestuurders verandert er dan iets essentieels: je volgt niet meer het stoplicht, maar het gedrag van de auto voor je. Remt die, dan rem jij; rijdt die door, dan kun je veilig meelopen in de stroom.

offbeat.title: Corruzione, chieste misure cautelari per il sindaco e il professionista
offbeat.summary: Il sindaco Pasquale Marrandino ‘Mazzette’ in cambio di incarichi pubblici ben remunerati. È quanto contestato dai sostituti procuratori Giacomo Urbano e Anna Ida Capone al sindaco di Castel Volturno Pasquale Marrandino e all’ingegnere Daniele De Caprio indagati per corruzione. Una scatolina bianca passata dal professionista all’interno della Jeep del primo cittadino e nascosta sotto il sedile e poi riaperta più volte per prelevare le banconote. E’ l’immagine ‘chiave’ dell’inchiesta dei carabinieri del nucleo operativo di Caserta che sette mesi fa ha portato a perquisizioni nelle abitazioni, uffici e automobili dei due indagati.

offbeat.title: 6 Emmerdale spoilers for tonight confirm Dr Todd death suspect
offbeat.summary: Emmerdale spoilers follow from Thursday's episode (23 July), which is available to watch now on ITVX and YouTube but hasn't aired on ITV1 yet.In tonight's Emmerdale, Vanessa and Mackenzie share their frustration over the lack of justice in Charity's case against Dr Todd. But one of them seems determined to take control of the situation…Meanwhile, Cain is furious when his camping trip with Kyle is hijacked by Graham – and the simmering tension has worrying consequences.Read on for six big moments from Thursday's episode…1. Mack tries to move onIt's a new day for Charity after Helen's decision not to go to the police with a formal complaint against Dr Todd.

offbeat.title: Suore lasciano dopo 75 anni, chiude la comunità delle Orsoline: «Crisi di vocazioni, mancano religiose disponibili»
offbeat.summary: Per settantacinque anni sono state una presenza discreta ma costante. Hanno visto crescere generazioni di bambini, accompagnato famiglie nei momenti di gioia e di dolore, insegnato il catechismo, pregato, ascoltato e aiutato chi bussava alla loro porta. Da settembre tutto questo diventerà un ricordo. Le Suore Orsoline Figlie di Maria Immacolata lasceranno Castelmassa, segnando un altro addio che colpisce il Polesine dopo la recente partenza delle religiose da Scardovari. Un epilogo che racconta non solo la fine di una lunga storia, ma anche la crisi vocazionale che sta cambiando il volto della Chiesa.

offbeat.title: Guardiola verso il no all'Italia: vuole 20 milioni l'anno, la corsa al ct si riapre
offbeat.summary: Per un’oretta ha potuto staccare la mente dal pensiero del ct. Giovanni Malagò ieri sera, a un passo dal Tevere, ha accantonato l’abito presidenziale, indossato la maglia del “suo” Circolo Aniene che ha guidato alla vittoria della Coppa Canottieri, torneo storico tra i circoli romani. Accanto a lui Roberto Mancini, autore di cinque gol, ma questa è un’altra storia. Malagò aspetta e ovviamente spera. E, ormai è cosa nota, l’uomo che vorrebbe tanto portare sulla panchina azzurra è Pep Guardiola. Quello per cui "si possono anche fare eccezioni" persino se si parla di ingaggi, nonostante il budget federale sia un problema dichiarato.

offbeat.title: Ashes 2027 schedule: England vs Australia men's series to start at Trent Bridge, women's at Headingley
offbeat.summary: England’s men’s Ashes campaign opens at Trent Bridge on 18 June 2027, with the women’s series starting at Headingley six days later. The men’s Tests will also be played at Lord’s, Edgbaston, Southampton and the Oval, while the women’s matches run from 24 June to 20 July. Both series will run concurrently as the teams chase Ashes glory on home soil.

offbeat.title: Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston and Kelsey Mitchell make WNBA history before All-Star Weekend
offbeat.summary: Caitlin Clark led the Indiana Fever to a historic 123-88 victory over the Connecticut Sun, marking the team's highest single-game score and tying for the third-highest in WNBA history. Clark scored 27 points and dished out 11 assists, while Kelsey Mitchell and Aliyah Boston added 23 and 22 points, respectively, making them all starters for the upcoming All-Star game. The Fever, now 17-10, head into the All-Star break on a three-game winning streak.

offbeat.title: Omicidio e tentato femminicidio a Quartu e Solanas, spara uccidendo il fratello e ferendo gravemente la moglie
offbeat.summary: Nel giro di poche ore ci sarebbero stati un omicidio e un tentato femminicidio nel Cagliaritano e il responsabile sarebbe la stessa persona. La vittima sarebbe un uomo di 43 anni, ucciso a Quartu. Prima di lui, il responsabile avrebbe ferito gravemente una donna di 48 anni a Solanas. Si sarebbe poi costituito alle forze dell’ordine, che interrogandolo avrebbero confermato che i due delitti sono collegati. Secondo le prime ricostruzioni, le vittime sarebbero la moglie e il fratello dell’uomo che si è costituito.

offbeat.title: Virginie Efira se livre sur sa seconde grossesse à 46 ans: "Je ne pensais vraiment plus avoir d'enfant"
offbeat.summary: Virginie Efira est une maman épanouie. Aujourd'hui en couple avec le comédien Niels Schneider , l'actrice belge est à la tête d'une famille de deux enfants. Le premier, Ali , est né en 2013 de sa relation avec le réalisateur Mabrouk El Mechri. Le second, Hiro , a lui été conçu dix ans plus tard avec son compagnon actuel. Pourtant, la native de Schaerbeek a longtemps cru construire sa vie avec une fille unique. La deuxième grossesse de la star de Revoir Paris n'était, en effet, pas prévue, comme elle l'explique dans Psychologie magazine. "J'avais 46 ans quand mon fils est né, je pensais vraiment ne plus avoir d'enfant. C'était une merveilleuse surprise" , confie-t-elle.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026: Gary Anderson thrashes Ryan Joyce to reach last-16
offbeat.summary: Gary Anderson dominated Ryan Joyce with a 10-2 victory to advance to the last-16 of the World Matchplay Darts in Blackpool. The 2018 champion showcased his skill with impressive finishes of 144 and 141, averaging nearly 110. Anderson will face Welsh fifth seed Jonny Clayton next, who defeated Damon Heta 10-7.

offbeat.title: Commissioner encourages reporting as concern over antisocial behaviour continues
offbeat.summary: Police and Crime Commissioner Alison Hernandez reports rising antisocial behaviour concerns in Devon and Cornwall towns like Looe, Brixham and Exmouth. Over the past year, authorities have issued 31 closure orders, 40 Community Protection Warnings and more than 40 Notices to address persistent nuisance. Today’s panel in Plymouth will review ongoing legal interventions to protect communities across the peninsula.

offbeat.title: ClickHouse announced as Principal Partner & Front of Shirt Sponsor
offbeat.summary: Fulham Football Club is proud to announce ClickHouse, a global technology company specialising in real-time analytics, data infrastructure and AI applications, as its newest Principal Partner and Front of Shirt Sponsor of the Men’s First Team.

offbeat.title: Vollsperrung auf der A1 - Lkw mit Kakaobohnen steht in Flammen
offbeat.summary: The A1 near Seevetal in Lower Saxony remains fully closed after a truck carrying cocoa beans caught fire around 9 a.m. today. The driver escaped unharmed but Glutnester in the cargo are still being extinguished, delaying reopening. Authorities expect prolonged southbound restrictions until the blaze is fully under control.

offbeat.title: HandOfBlood will not be at gamescom 2026, the reason meets with much agreement: "Used to be a real games fair"
offbeat.summary: Top German gaming influencer Max “HandOfBlood” Knabe will miss gamescom 2026, his management confirms. Influencer crowd chaos at past events—like MontanaBlack’s 2022 appearance—prompted the move. Organizers cite stricter accreditation rules as the fair shifts back to games over influencer spectacle.

offbeat.title: 'Ter Stegens komst is kwestie van tijd; Ounahi blijkt toch best wel lastig verhaal'
offbeat.summary: Ajax expects Marc-André ter Stegen’s move to be finalized soon, with the German goalkeeper still linked to the Amsterdam club. Negotiations between Ter Stegen and Barcelona remain the final hurdle, delaying Ajax’s defensive rebuild. The club remains confident a deal will be completed despite ongoing fiscal discussions.

offbeat.title: Darwin Núñez acelera su salida de Saudí con la vista puesta en el Barça
offbeat.summary: El delantero Darwin Núñez está ya forzando la máquina para salir del Al Hilal en este mercado de fichajes. Ni el club saudí cuenta con él, ni el jugador uruguayo está por la labor de continuar en un equipo que ha apostado claramente por Karim Benzema dejándole en el banquillo. Núñez se incorporó este martes a la concentración del equipo en Austria y espera solventar su situación antes del 3 de agosto, día en el que todo el grupo debe regresar ya para Arabia.

offbeat.title: Third person dies from NYC Legionnaires’ outbreak
offbeat.summary: A third person has died from the Legionnaires’ disease outbreak on New York City’s Upper East Side, health officials confirmed Sunday. The cluster has sickened at least 74 people, with eight still hospitalized as of Sunday night. Officials say the decline in new cases suggests the source of the outbreak has likely been contained.

offbeat.title: Le nouveau Rafale F5 et le grand drone de combat, priorités de Dassault Aviation
offbeat.summary: Dassault Aviation confirms the Rafale F5 and a new combat drone as top priorities after France and Germany ended the Future Air Combat System project. The NGF fighter jet, once central to the program, will now proceed independently with interoperability for allied aircraft. Development on the combat cloud component has been halted following the joint decision last month.

offbeat.title: Honourable defeat might be the best Mayo can hope for against Kerry
offbeat.summary: In the aftermath of last Sunday , we gathered and tried to pinpoint just where exactly this lay on the ever-growing list of All-Ireland final defeats we’d seen Galway suffer. We cycled through some options. Nothing will ever be worse than the 2024 football final – that’s a given (our generation’s 1983). The supremacy of Limerick in every facet of last Sunday’s hurling decider meant there was no one moment you could look at as a turning point. There was no disputed late free, as there was for the footballers in the 2022 final against Kerry, for instance.

offbeat.title: Kürzungen bei armen Rentnern und Familien: Verband warnt vor Regierungsplänen
offbeat.summary: Die Bundesregierung plant Kürzungen beim Wohngeld, die vor allem Familien mit Kindern, Geringverdiener und Rentner mit kleinen Renten treffen würden. Laut einer Kurzexpertise des Paritätischen Wohlfahrtsverbands wären 70 Prozent der Wohngeldempfänger Haushalte mit Kindern und 15 Prozent Rentner. Betroffene warnen, dass weitere Kürzungen die ohnehin prekäre finanzielle Situation verschärfen würden.

offbeat.title: Al Hilal open talks to sign Ousmane Dembélé from PSG
offbeat.summary: Al Hilal has initiated talks to sign Ousmane Dembélé from Paris Saint-Germain as part of their strategy to enhance their squad during the summer transfer window. The Saudi club has identified the 28-year-old forward as a key target and discussions with his representatives are already underway. Meanwhile, PSG is keen to extend Dembélé's contract, emphasizing their desire to retain the player who has become central to their attacking lineup.

offbeat.title: ‘He knows what he’s doing’: Burnham’s off-the-cuff remarks inspire headlines and headaches
offbeat.summary: Britain’s Prime Minister Andy Burnham sparked fresh debate on Wednesday after suggesting higher earners may face tax rises while ruling out an income tax threshold increase. His off-the-cuff remarks came just three days into office, testing his promise to do politics differently. Downing Street is expected to clarify the policy platform by the end of the week.

offbeat.title: Around 12,000 people evacuated in France fires
offbeat.summary: Around 12,000 people have been evacuated in France’s Gironde region as wildfires rage, with over 2,000 hectares burned. No injuries have been reported, though authorities have banned engine use in forested areas and closed roads. Meanwhile, Italy mourns a firefighter killed battling fires in Sicily, where more than 350 blazes have prompted evacuations.

offbeat.title: The millennial generation has split, new Fed research shows: those over 35 are edging toward boomer-style wealth, while everyone else falls behind
offbeat.summary: Millennials over 35 are closing in on boomer-era homeownership levels, but those under 35 now own homes at just 22%, according to new Federal Reserve research. The traditional homeownership rate of 37% for under-35s only counts household heads, masking the true gap. The Fed’s new measure shows national homeownership at 53%, far below the widely cited 65%.

offbeat.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
offbeat.summary: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike today, losing 11 kilograms after demanding government action on education reforms. Protests over exam paper leaks have intensified pressure on Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government, which now faces calls to address systemic failures. Authorities say fast-track courts will be set up to punish those responsible for leaks.

offbeat.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
offbeat.summary: Ukrainian drone strikes have hit two Wildberries warehouses in Russia, killing eight workers and disrupting supplies for hundreds of small businesses. The attacks in Elektrostal and Kotovsk follow Kyiv’s shift to targeting logistics hubs used by Russia’s military. Thousands of sellers now face financial losses as the war’s impact spreads closer to home.

facts.title: River levels approach all-time low as drought announcement looms
facts.summary: River levels in Wales are approaching record lows as drought conditions intensify, with July rainfall at just 5% of the average amid ongoing heatwaves. Natural Resources Wales is set to confirm an environmental drought for parts of the country later today. Farmers report failing crops and heat-stressed livestock as wildlife suffers from critically low river flows.

facts.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
facts.summary: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station captured a stunning panorama of Washington state’s Olympic Mountains on July 23, 2026. The image highlights the rugged peaks, deep river valleys, and alpine glaciers of Olympic National Park. The region’s unique geology, formed from ancient undersea eruptions, remains a remote wilderness surrounded by coastal waters and islands.

facts.title: Butterfly city blooms in shadow of office blocks
facts.summary: A mini meadow in Hull city centre has become a haven for ten butterfly species, including the rare small skipper. The project, launched in 2023 near St Stephen’s Shopping Centre, allows grass and wildflowers to grow undisturbed to support caterpillars. Organisers plan to expand the scheme to more urban sites next year.

entertainment.title: The music in Nolan’s Odyssey is as well-crafted – and bloodlessly unmemorable – as the movie itself
entertainment.summary: Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey features a score by Ludwig Göransson that draws on 2,000-year-old Greek musical research. The soundtrack’s aulos theme blends reconstructed ancient melodies with modern film scoring. Critics note the music enhances the film’s epic scale without overshadowing its visuals.

entertainment.title: Daniel Wu on ‘Cold War 1994,’ ‘Twilight of the Warriors’ and Coming Home to Hong Kong: ‘The Craft Still Challenges Me’
entertainment.summary: Daniel Wu stars in Cold War 1994, the third installment of the highest-grossing Hong Kong franchise, premiering at the New York Asian Film Festival. The prequel, set before the 1997 handover, reunites Wu with three generations of Hong Kong actors. Wu returns to Hong Kong filmmaking after pandemic delays, calling the production’s scale and script a key draw.

entertainment.title: Venice Film Festival Reveals 2026 Lineup (Updating Live)
entertainment.summary: The Venice Film Festival unveiled its 2026 lineup today, opening with Danny Boyle’s “Ink,” a biographical drama about Rupert Murdoch starring Guy Pearce and Jack O’Connell. Netflix acquired U.S. rights to the film, which will compete for the Golden Lion. Italian director Giovanni Veronesi’s “Dio Ride” closes the festival outside the competition.

sports.title: 'Even the CEO doesn't know' - Kaymer on LIV's future
sports.summary: Martin Kaymer says LIV Golf’s 2024 team championship in Michigan is “highly unlikely” as Saudi backing ends this season after £3.75bn of investment. CEO Scott O’Neil is seeking $350m to keep the tour alive but admits even he lacks clarity on 2027 plans. The next event starts Thursday at JCB Club in Staffordshire ahead of stops in New York and Indianapolis.

sports.title: No 2027 men's Ashes Test in north of England
sports.summary: England’s men’s Ashes series against Australia next year will begin at Trent Bridge on 18 June, with the women’s series starting six days later. The men’s Tests will run through July at Lord’s, Edgbaston, Southampton and the Oval, but no matches are scheduled in the north of England. England will open their campaign with a Test against Bangladesh at Lord’s from 28 May.

sports.title: No men's Ashes Test in north of England - full 2027 schedule
sports.summary: England’s men’s Ashes campaign starts at Trent Bridge on 18 June 2027, with no Tests in the north of England after Old Trafford and Headingley were left out. The five-Test series runs alongside the women’s Ashes from 24 June to 20 July, ending at the Kia Oval on 29 July. Australia will warm up against England Lions at Old Trafford from 7 June before the opening Test.

breaking.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
breaking.summary: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a landmark nuclear cooperation deal on Wednesday, allowing Riyadh to develop a civilian nuclear program with expanded access for American companies. The agreement includes a bilateral safeguards pact to prevent proliferation, though details remain undisclosed. Experts warn the deal could set a precedent for uranium enrichment in the region.

breaking.title: U.S.-Saudi Deal Prompts Fears of a Nuclear Arms Race
breaking.summary: In Europe, Asia and the Middle East, countries seek nuclear capabilities to hedge against aggressive neighbors and a receding Washington, potentially creating a more freewheeling era.

breaking.title: A New Target for Ukraine’s Long-Range Attacks: Russia’s Version of Amazon
breaking.summary: Kyiv is escalating its air war by striking commercial warehouses, trying to bring the conflict closer to home for ordinary Russians.

tech.title: Framework’s premium laptop is shipping with less RAM
tech.summary: Framework is raising prices and adjusting RAM on its Laptop 13 Pro after a supplier cost surge, with prebuilt models now starting at $1,599. Some preorder customers face revised memory configurations and higher prices, though Framework offers full refunds for cancellations. New orders reflect the updated pricing and specs.

tech.title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
tech.summary: It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.

tech.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
tech.summary: Alphabet reported a negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion in its latest quarter, its first deficit in over a decade, as AI infrastructure costs surged to $45 billion. Revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but stock fell 4% after hours. The company now expects AI spending to reach $205 billion this year.

politics.title: UK millionaires ask Andy Burnham to tax them more
politics.summary: UK millionaires including Gary Lineker and Brian Eno have urged Prime Minister Andy Burnham to raise taxes on the wealthiest. A group of 120 signatories proposed a 2% tax on fortunes over £10m to fund public services. Burnham has not ruled out a wealth tax but has given no further details.

politics.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
politics.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

politics.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
politics.summary: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

crisis.title: Government to cut business rates for pubs, clubs and music venues
crisis.summary: Pubs, clubs and music venues in England will receive a 20% cut to business rates from April, saving firms around £1,100 each next year. The government estimates the move will support nearly 32,000 venues at a cost of £100m. Details on eligibility, excluding the largest live music venues, will be confirmed at the autumn Budget.

crisis.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
crisis.summary: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

crisis.title: As Cyclosporiasis Spreads, America Says No Thanks to Salads
crisis.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

health.title: Alarm over hospital lung admissions for children in England’s poorest postcodes
health.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections than those in wealthier postcodes, new NHS data shows. In 2024/25, 1,941 per 100,000 children in the most deprived areas were admitted compared to 1,380 per 100,000 in the least deprived. Experts warn air pollution and deprivation are key drivers, with calls for urgent action to address the disparity.

health.title: Life-changing MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
health.summary: The NHS in England will now offer fampridine, the first drug to help some people with multiple sclerosis walk, to up to 5,000 patients annually. The treatment, which boosts nerve signals to improve mobility, follows clinical trials showing walking benefits for four in ten users. NHS England confirmed the drug’s inclusion after private costs became unsustainable for many patients.
